facts.notes: NY splits by region: $17.00 in NYC / Long Island / Westchester, $16.00 rest-of-state. The STATE jurisdiction row carries the statewide floor ($16.00); the downstate uplift belongs on the city/county rows. Deliberately `warn`, not `critical` — a catalog that stored $17.00 here is arguably reasonable, and a human should adjudicate rather than have accuracy zeroed.
Rest-of-state tipped FOOD SERVICE cash wage ($10.70 cash + $5.30 credit). Downstate is $11.35 + $5.65. Service employees use different figures again. Only hospitality employers may take a tip credit.
Rest-of-state executive/administrative threshold ($1,199.10/wk). Downstate is $1,275.00/wk = $66,300/yr. NY sets no separate professional threshold (follows FLSA).
12 NYCRR 142-2.2 — 1.5x after 40 hrs (44 for residential employees). NY has no daily-overtime rule.
NY Labor Law 167 — a health care employer may not require a nurse to work beyond regularly scheduled hours, with narrow emergency exceptions. Voluntary overtime remains allowed. Healthcare pack.
12 NYCRR 142-2.4 — one extra hour at the BASIC MINIMUM wage (not the employee's rate) when the spread exceeds 10 hours, or on a split shift.
12 NYCRR 142-2.3 call-in pay: at least four hours, or the scheduled shift length if shorter, at the basic minimum wage.

NY Labor Law 196-b, tiered by size and net income: 100+ employees = 56 hrs paid; 5-99 = 40 hrs paid; 0-4 with >$1M net income = 40 hrs paid; 0-4 otherwise = 40 hrs unpaid. The substring targets the top tier.
2026: 12 weeks at 67% of AWW, capped at 67% of NYSAWW ($1,228.53/wk max benefit); employee contribution 0.432% of gross wages, annual cap $411.91. Contribution rate is reset annually by DFS.
NY Labor Law 194-b — 4+ employees must disclose a compensation range in job, promotion, and transfer postings, including remote roles reporting to a NY worksite.
